On this page

On this page

  • Overview
  • Category index convention
  • Autogenerated sidebar metadata
    • Doc item metadata
    • Category item metadata

Autogenerated

Autogenerated

Section titled “Autogenerated”

Docusaurus can create a sidebar automatically from your filesystem structure:

  • each folder creates a sidebar category
  • each file creates a doc link.

An autogenerated item is converted by Docusaurus to a sidebar slice so you can splice multiple autogenerated items from multiple directories, interleaving them with regular sidebar items, in one sidebar level.

Example

Consider this file structure:

sidebar definition:

It would be resolved as:

Note how the autogenerate source directories themselves don’t become categories : only the items they contain do. This is what we mean by “sidebar slice”.

Category index convention

Section titled “Category index convention”

Docusaurus can automatically link a category to its index document .

Note

Normally (without link or category index) when category is clicked, nothing happend

A category index document is a document following one of those filename conventions:

  • Named as index (case-insensitive): docs/Guides/index.md
  • Named as README (case-insensitive): docs/Guides/README.mdx
  • Same name as parent folder: docs/Guides/Guides.md

This is equivalent to using a category with a doc link.

Naming your introductory document README.md makes it show up when browsing the folder using the GitHub interface, while using index.md makes the behavior more in line with how HTML files are served.

Note

If a folder only has one index page, it will be turned into a link instead of a category.

Autogenerated sidebar metadata

Section titled “Autogenerated sidebar metadata”

For handwritten sidebar definitions, you would provide metadata to sidebar items through sidebars.js.

For autogenerated, Docusaurus would read them from the item’s respective file.

In addition, you may want to adjust the relative position of each item because, by default, items within a sidebar slice will be generated in alphabetical order (using file and folder names).

Doc item metadata

Section titled “Doc item metadata”

The label, className, and customProps attributes are declared in front matter as sidebar_label, sidebar_class_name, and sidebar_custom_props, respectively.

Front mattersidebar.js
sidebar_labellabel
sidebar_class_nameclassName
sidebar_custom_propscustomProps

Position can be specified in the same way, via sidebar_position front matter.

Category item metadata

Section titled “Category item metadata”

By adding a _category_.json or _category_.yml file in the respective folder.

You can specify any category metadata and also the position metadata.

label, className, position, and customProps will default to the respective values of the category’s linked doc, if there is one.

  • JSON
  • YAML

Explicit link behavior

If the link is explicitly specified, Docusaurus will not apply any default conventions.

The doc links can be specified relatively. For example, if the category is generated with the guides directory, "link": {"type": "doc", "id": "intro"} will be resolved to the ID guides/intro, only falling back to intro if a doc with the former ID doesn’t exist.

You can also use link: null to opt out of default conventions and not generate any category index page.

Note

The position metadata is only used within a sidebar slice: Docusaurus does not re-order other items of your sidebar.


Sources

Section titled “Sources”
  • https://docusaurus.io/docs/sidebar/autogenerated

Last updated: Sep 28, 2025
